title: Path Param vs Query Param
prompt_md: Consider the request `GET /users/42`. What is the `42`?
options: A path parameter identifying which user
explanation_md: The 42 is a path parameter — it's embedded in the URL path and identifies *which* specific resource (user #42). Query parameters come after a `?` (like `/users?role=admin`) and modify how a collection is viewed rather than identifying one item. Headers and bodies are separate parts of the request entirely; GET requests typically have no body.
